figures.caption: Figure 1. (a) Comparison of AUC and MACs of recent SOTA trackers. (b) Baseline models decline after the 5th frame due to visual redundancy on LaSOT, but our ATC module overcomes this, enabling sustained performance gains with more frames. (c) Impact of ATC module and template token count on LaSOT AUC(%). (d) Efficiency Gains from the ATC module.

Table 1. Performance comparison with SOTA trackers on the test sets of GOT-10k [25], LaSOT [12], LaSOText [13], TrackingNet [45], and TNL2K [55]. For GOT-10k, an asterisk (*) denotes models trained exclusively on its training set. MCITrack-B224† was locally trained under identical model, GPU, and configuration conditions. The top two results are highlighted with bold and underlined fonts, respectively.

Figure 2. (a) ETCTrack Framework Architecture. The process begins with our ATC module, which effectively compresses visual tokens from historical template frames to eliminate visual redundancy. These compressed tokens, along with search region tokens, are then fed into the Hierarchical Interaction Encoder for contextual feature interaction. Finally, the enhanced search features are sent to the Prediction Head to predict bounding box. (b) The detailed architecture of our core ATC module.
Figure 3. The Mask-Guided Token Pruning and Merging Module.
Figure 7. Visualization of visual redundancy elimination.
Figure 4. The structure of the Hierarchical Interaction Block.
Figure 5. AUC scores of different attributes on LaSOT [12].
